label: Recommended First Two Years by Major
source: Recommended Courses for First 2 Years by Major 2026
note: Each entry's match_major_codes is compared against selected programs' major_code field. match_program_ids allows finer-grained matching when two programs share a major_code (e.g., two KIN programs). Strings in essential/suggested that match PREFIX-NUM are checked against taken courses; other strings are shown as descriptive text.

entries.label: Biology — standard track
entries.variant_note: Default track for Biology majors. CHM sequence begins sophomore year unless pre-med.
entries.notes: BIO-235 is the prime prerequisite for most upper-level biology electives and must be completed by end of sophomore year. MTH-135 or STA-100/110 can be taken any semester during the first two years. 11 courses needed for the major.

entries.label: Biology — advanced track
entries.variant_note: For pre-med students and those with strong high school science background (ACT ≥ 25). CHM sequence begins immediately.
entries.notes: CHM-121 must begin first semester — the chemistry sequence (121 → 122 → 221 → 321) must finish before MCAT prep junior year. BIO-235 must be completed by end of sophomore year. 11 courses needed for the major.

entries.label: Biology — typical track
entries.variant_note: For students with limited high school science background. Fall 2026: use BIO-100 instead of BIO-120 (BIO-120 not offered).
entries.notes: BIO-235 must be completed by end of sophomore year. Progress through CHM-121/122 as quickly as possible.

entries.label: Business Administration
entries.notes: 11 courses needed for the major. Most majors complete at least 5 classes in the major by end of sophomore year. Students not starting in Year 1 can still finish in 4 years but will need to double up more.

entries.label: Chemistry
entries.notes: Students earning 4-5 on AP Chemistry may start with CHM-122/122L in Year 1 Fall. CHM-221 offered fall only; CHM-321 spring only. ACS certification requires CHM-121/121L and MTH-135 in fall. Complete MTH-145 by end of sophomore year.

entries.label: Communication Studies
entries.notes: The COM major is flexible. 100-level for first-year students, 200-level for sophomores. Students may be ready for 300-level in sophomore year if they have two COM courses beyond COM-101.

entries.label: Computer Science
entries.notes: Students earning 4-5 on AP Computer Science A earn credit for CS-125. Students with programming experience should consult faculty about placement options.

entries.label: Creative Writing
entries.notes: 10 courses needed. Complete CRW-125, ENG-101, and a beginning (200-level) creative writing workshop by end of sophomore year. Majors need beginning workshops in at least two genres and must take 3 English classes including ENG-101 and one numbered ENG-281 or above.

entries.label: Economics / International Economics
entries.notes: Best to complete ECO-175 and ECO-215 in Year 1. Students considering a graduate degree should begin calculus in Year 1 or 2. Many economics majors also complete a math major or minor.

entries.label: Elementary Education
entries.notes: Apply to Coe Teacher Education after any two core courses. All majors need at least one teaching endorsement area — nearly all choose Reading (begins with EDU-241 in Y2). All majors must take an American History class and a science with a lab.

entries.label: English
entries.notes: 10 courses needed. Complete ENG-101, ENG-281, ENG-291 and a lower-level elective by end of sophomore year. ENG-281 offered spring only; ENG-291 fall only. Take ENG-301 in year 2 or 3 before moving deeply into 305+ courses.

entries.label: Environmental Studies (collateral)
entries.notes: Collateral major — students typically do not start it in first term. Contact program coordinator Dr. Ellis during the first year. Biology majors must take MTH-135 for this collateral. Chemistry majors should also take BIO-155 in spring of Year 1 (prerequisite for BIO-295).

entries.label: Interdisciplinary French & Francophone Studies
entries.notes: Must pass a Spanish placement test or receive instructor approval for initial placement. Students should work toward intermediate-level proficiency before junior year study abroad.

entries.label: Health & Society Studies (minor)
entries.notes: Required: PHL-165, SOC-107, one bio course, and three electives from the approved list. Only one 100-level elective allowed.

entries.label: History
entries.notes: 10 courses required. History courses generally have no prerequisites, so first-year students may take 200 or 300-level courses of interest. Plan to take HIS-205 in Fall of sophomore year.

entries.label: International Studies

essential: Foreign language course (especially if starting new language)
Next foreign language course in sequence

entries.notes: Must pass two foreign language courses at the intermediate level. Makes most sense to complete before junior year abroad. Starting a new language in Year 1 Fall is optimal to reach intermediate before going abroad.

entries.label: Kinesiology — Pre-Athletic Training
entries.notes: BIO-155 must be completed by end of sophomore year.

entries.label: Mathematics
entries.notes: AP Calculus AB score ≥ 4 earns credit for MTH-135. AP Calculus BC score ≥ 4 earns credit for MTH-145 and MTH-135. CS-125 must be completed before graduation. Consult mathematics faculty about appropriate calculus placement level.

entries.label: Museum Studies (minor)
entries.notes: Students should meet a faculty member in the Art and Visual Studies Department to discuss appropriate courses.

entries.label: Music (Bachelor of Arts)

suggested: MU-284 (Topics: Rock & Roll or Taylor Swift)

entries.notes: Contact Dr. Carson or the advising director with questions. Music Education majors should take EDU-105 and EDU-195 early.

entries.label: Nursing (BSN)
entries.notes: NUR-100/200 are 0.2cc courses taken fall and spring. Apply to the nursing program in December of sophomore year (NUR-200). Requires GPA ≥ 2.7 and 16 cc completed by end of sophomore year. All Gen Eds (except WE) must be complete by end of sophomore year. Students will receive a Nursing Advisor at the start of freshman year.

entries.label: Philosophy
entries.notes: 9 courses needed. 100-level courses are especially well-suited for first-year students. 200-level for students who have already taken a 100-level course or completed their first year. 300-level courses have prerequisites.

entries.label: Physics
entries.notes: Participate in May Term Advanced Laboratory and summer research between Years 1 and 2. Physics uses calculus-based General Physics I — it is exceedingly rare to skip this even with AP credit.

entries.label: Political Science

suggested: Political science course with prerequisites met

entries.notes: 10 courses needed. Complete at least 4 classes in the major by end of sophomore year. Not advisable to take a 300-level course before spring semester sophomore year.

entries.label: Psychology
entries.notes: Applied Career Development in Psychology is a good choice for students seeking to explore or intending an internship (PSY-494 requires it). Org Science majors should take PSY-245 in Year 1 or 2. Neuroscience majors should take PSY-250 in sophomore year.

entries.label: Social & Criminal Justice

essential: SCJ-101 or SCJ-203 (if SCJ-101 taken fall)

entries.notes: 10 courses needed (3 are electives). Several courses on every-other-year basis (PHL-265/285, SOC-351). SOC-107 is NOT required for SOC-235 if SCJ-101 has been taken.

entries.label: Sociology
entries.notes: 10 courses needed. SOC-107 (or transfer credit/instructor consent) is a prerequisite for all other SOC courses. Students considering a 300-level course before spring sophomore year should seek departmental feedback.

entries.label: Studio Art
entries.notes: For the art major, students need to complete two 100, two 200, two 300, and two 400-level ART courses and three ARH courses. Meet with a faculty member in Art and Visual Studies to discuss course selection.

entries.label: Art History (minor)
entries.notes: Meet with a faculty member in the Art and Visual Studies Department to discuss appropriate courses.

entries.label: Organizational Science (collateral)
entries.notes: Concurrent completion of the Psychology major is required. See also the Psychology first-two-years plan.
